---
title: "Simulation check"
---

# Simulation check {#sec-simulation-check}

This chapter is a sanity check, not a simulation study. It builds a
small synthetic data-generating process with known true
$\boldsymbol\beta(\mathbf Z)$, fits `scfit()`, and compares the
recovered per-respondent preference vector to the truth. The goal
is pedagogical: to give readers confidence that the estimator works
on data whose ground truth they can inspect directly.

```{r}
library(sconjoint)
set.seed(1)
```

The DGP below draws 300 respondents, five tasks, three binary
attributes, and two continuous covariates, and simulates
$\hat\beta(\mathbf Z)$ through a simple linear function of
$\mathbf Z$. Users who want a full simulation study — with Monte
Carlo replications, coverage calculations, and DGPs that stress the
estimator — should look instead at the private prototype scripts
that ship with the paper replication materials.

```{r}
cat("simulation check\n")
```
